Description: Free metasearch engine aggregating results from multiple search services and databases.
Evidence: Public implementation; active canonical repository; direct web research use case.
Notes: Accepted after verification of canonical repository and direct web research use case.

Description: Python perceptual image hashing module for similarity comparison.
Evidence: Maintained canonical ImageHash package; supports perceptual image matching workflows.
Notes: Accepted as the maintained canonical ImageHash package despite its historical fork relationship to photohash.

Description: Python face detection and alignment library.
Evidence: Public implementation; face detection and alignment are documented investigation-support capabilities.
Notes: Accepted as an image-analysis support library with a documented investigation use case.

Description: Python face recognition and facial attribute analysis framework.
Evidence: Public implementation documents verification, recognition, search, and analysis functions.
Notes: Accepted as an image-analysis framework with documented verification and search functions.

Description: HTTP toolkit for running high-volume probes against web targets.
Evidence: Active canonical repository; tagged OSINT; practical web asset reconnaissance use case.
Notes: Accepted as an active web asset reconnaissance tool.

Description: Secret scanner for Git repositories and other inputs.
Evidence: Public implementation; remote Git repository scanning and GitHub workflow support are documented.
Notes: Accepted for public repository exposure research; upstream states feature-complete maintenance mode.

Description: Finds, verifies, and analyzes exposed credentials across supported sources.
Evidence: Public implementation documents GitHub organization, user, repository, and related-source scanning.
Notes: Accepted for documented GitHub organization, user, and repository scanning.

Description: Browser library for rendering interactive node-and-edge network visualizations.
Evidence: Implementation is generic visualization infrastructure without a direct OSINT or investigative workflow.
Notes: Rejected: generic graph visualization library without a direct OSINT or investigative workflow.

Description: Extensible secrets scanner maintained by the original Gitleaks team.
Evidence: Gitleaks names it as the successor; supports GitHub, GitLab, Git, filesystems, and extensible sources.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after Gitleaks identified it as the actively developed successor.

Description: Extracts email addresses, phone numbers, and social profile links from websites.
Evidence: Public implementation includes crawl, contact extraction, social extraction, and structured export modes.
Notes: Accepted as a focused website contact and profile extraction tool.

Description: Collects public TikTok profile metadata from a supplied username.
Evidence: Implementation-bearing fork with twelve commits beyond Dutchosintguy/TikTokOSINT; directly referenced by the supplied text.
Notes: Accepted as the implementation-bearing fork directly cited in the supplied text; fork relationship retained in metadata.

Description: Collects TikTok search, user, tag, and media data through SerpAPI and Apify.
Evidence: Public CLI and web implementation with documented SerpAPI, Apify, and yt-dlp collection workflows.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as an active API-assisted TikTok collection project.

Description: Combines multi-target collection, entity correlation, graphs, and reporting in a desktop application.
Evidence: Repository contains a multi-module Python implementation rather than a landing page or stub.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after implementation tree verification.

Description: Provides username scanning, social scraping, phone lookup, and search-query generation modules.
Evidence: Repository contains executable modules for username, phone, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube workflows.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging after implementation tree verification.

Description: Collects exposed email addresses from supported social platforms and checks related leak data.
Evidence: Public Rust implementation documents Instagram, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, and PwnDB-related workflows.
Notes: Accepted as a cross-platform social email collection tool; leak-source use requires operator policy review.

Description: Downloads image galleries and media collections from supported websites.
Evidence: GitHub README identifies Codeberg as the canonical active-development repository; implementation and current commits verified there.
Notes: Accepted using the canonical Codeberg repository identified by the project's GitHub migration notice.

Description: Collects X timelines and search results through Playwright with resume and proxy controls.
Evidence: Public implementation matches the feature description in the supplied GitHub Topics reconnaissance.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as the repository matching the supplied GitHub Topics description.

Description: Collects public Twitter data through the frontend API without official API authentication.
Evidence: Canonical high-adoption repository matching the ambiguous twitter-scraper name; archived but historically relevant.
Notes: Accepted as the canonical high-adoption repository matching the text; archived status is explicit.

Description: Provides an unofficial Python library for collecting Twitter posts and user records.
Evidence: Public implementation supports searches by phrase and ID plus user collection; project is stale but not archived.
Notes: Accepted as a historical implementation; stale update date remains visible.

Description: Collects public Twitter posts, profiles, and account relationships without the official API.
Evidence: Widely used historical OSINT implementation; repository is archived and retained for reference.
Notes: Accepted for historical OSINT relevance with archived status explicit.

Description: Investigates usernames, email addresses, phone numbers, and names without mandatory API keys.
Evidence: Small but implementation-bearing multi-target OSINT project with documented confidence scoring.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a small implementation-bearing multi-target project.

Description: Searches phone-number variations across search engines and correlates identity clues.
Evidence: Public Python implementation documents multi-engine phone search and correlation.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a focused phone-number investigation project.

Description: Searches phone numbers across public engines and people-search sources in a Rust CLI.
Evidence: Distinct Rust implementation from the same maintainer with a documented phone OSINT workflow.
Notes: Accepted into Emerging as a distinct Rust phone-number investigation implementation.

Description: Curated list of username-search tools and online services.
Evidence: Repository is explicitly a curated source list without its own checker implementation.
Notes: Rejected: curated link and resource list without its own checker implementation.
